doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Solid pulmonary nodules in the left lower lobe, measuring up to 3 mm in diameter. Discoid atelectasis in the left upper lobe and right lower lobe. Lungs are unremarkable. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial effusion measuring up to 5 mm in thickness. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy. In the mid portion of the right kidney, a round hypodense lesion measures up to 6 mm in diameter, with density of -34 HU.
doc_impression:
1. Pericardial effusion measuring up to 5 mm.

2. Solid pulmonary nodules in the left lower lobe measuring up to 3 mm. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, no further CT follow-up is required.

3. Discoid atelectasis in both lungs.

4. Hypodense lesion in the right kidney (likely a benign cyst; no further follow-up is required).

5. Cholecystectomy.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Discoid atelectasis in the left upper lobe and right lower lobe, paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced pulmonary fibrosis in the right lower lobe, clinically insignificant. In the upper lobe of the left lung there is a subpleural ground-glass area, may be post-inflammatory or interstitial changes.
In the left lower lobe:
- Subpleural solid nodule measures 4 mm (series 3, image 160).
- Subpleural solid nodule measures 4 mm (series 3, image 144).
In the right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 4 mm (series 3, image 109).
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. 6 mm osteosclerotic lesion of T12 vertebral body. 

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy. In the mid portion of the right kidney, a round hypodense lesion measures up to 6 mm in diameter, with density of -34 HU.
val_impression:
1. Solid pulmonary nodules in both lungs measure up to 4 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.
In the upper lobe of the left lung there is a subpleural ground-glass area, may be post-inflammatory or interstitial changes.

2. Fat containing lesion in the right kidney. ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273) suggest the following: Benign uncomplicated angiomyolipoma. No need for further work-up.


DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 18.10 mGy; total DLP 661.68 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Micronodules in the right middle lobe and left upper lobe measure up to 3 mm. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the spine.

Upper abdomen: In the medial limb of the left adrenal gland, there is a lesion measuring 1.4 × 1.2 cm on axial images with macroscopic fat within the lesion, likely an adenoma. Cholecystectomy.

1.

An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2.

Left adrenal lesion. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: Benign adenoma. No need for further imaging follow-up. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status.

Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

4.

Cholecystectomy.

val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: In the upper lobe of the left lung, a ground glass area is identified adjacent to the interlobar pleura. In the upper lobe of the left lung, solid nodules are determined against the background of ground glass as follows: 
- in the image 22, series 5, a nodule measures 2 mm.
- in the image 28, series 5, a subpleural nodule measures 2 mm.
- in the image 29, series 5, a perifissural nodule measures 3 mm, likely a perifissural node.
- in the image 31, series 5, a subpleural nodule measures 2 mm.
- in the image 38, series 5, a perifissural nodule measures 3 mm, likely a perifissural node.
In other lobes of the lungs there are a few scattered micronodules of 2-3 mm. No consolidation or pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the spine.

Upper abdomen: In the medial limb of the left adrenal gland, there is a lesion measuring 1.4 × 1.2 cm, -13 HU, likely an adenoma. Cholecystectomy.
val_impression:
1. In the upper lobe of the left lung, a ground glass area and multiple small solid nodules may be residual changes after an inflammatory/infectious process. A few scattered micronodules of both lungs. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Left adrenal lesion. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: Benign adenoma. No need for further imaging follow-up. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: History of shortness of breath 5 months ago, now asymptomatic. 

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 14.11 mGy; total DLP 467 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: A nodule up to 2 mm in diameter is present in the left upper lobe, and a calcified nodule up to 3 mm in diameter is present in the lower lobe, stable compared with CT of the chest from July 26, 2019.  
Regression of reticular changes in the posterior portions of both lungs.  
The right lung is decreased in size due to total atelectasis of the middle lobe and subtotal atelectasis of the lower lobe. Traction bronchiectasis in the right lower lobe. Small calcifications along the diaphragmatic pleura. Discoid atelectasis in lung bases of the left lung.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The pulmonary trunk is not dilated. The main pulmonary arteries are dilated bilaterally, up to 2.5 cm on the right and up to 2.9 cm on the left.

Bones and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included upper abdomen: Eventration of the right hemidiaphragm. The gallbladder contains 8 gallstones, closely clustered, maximum size 1.4 × 1.2 cm. In the upper pole of the right kidney, there are two hypodense round homogeneous lesions, maximum size up to 1.0 cm in diameter, density 8 HU. In the liver, there are two hypodense round homogeneous lesions, maximum size up to 1.1 cm in diameter, density -8 HU (segment 2).
doc_impression:
1. Micronodule in the left upper lobe, stable compared with CT of the chest from July 26, 2019. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended. Calcified nodule in the left lower lobe, not requiring follow-up.

2. Regression of reticular changes in the posterior portions of both lungs compared with CT of the chest from July 26, 2019.

3. Total atelectasis of the right middle lobe and subtotal atelectasis of the right lower lobe. Traction bronchiectasis in the right lower lobe. Pleural calcifications on the right.

4. Dilated main pulmonary arteries. This may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

5. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

6. Eventration of the right hemidiaphragm.

7. Cholelithiasis.

8. Hypodense lesions in the right kidney (likely benign cysts, not requiring follow-up).

9. Hypodense lesions in the liver (likely benign cysts, not requiring follow-up).
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: A solid nodule measures 2 mm and is present in the left upper lobe (image 95), and a calcified nodule up to 3 mm in diameter is present in the left lower lobe (image 128), stable compared to Chest CT on July 26, 2019. 
The right lung is decreased in size due to total atelectasis of the middle lobe (negative dynamics, atelectasis was not previously seen) and subtotal atelectasis of the lower lobe. Discoid atelectasis in the lung bases of the left lung. Traction bronchiectasis in the right lower lobe. Small calcifications along the diaphragmatic pleura. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is qualitatively moderate.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The pulmonary trunk is not dilated. The left pulmonary artery is dilated up to 2.9 cm.

Bones and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included upper abdomen: Eventration of the right hemidiaphragm. The gallbladder contains 8 gallstones, closely clustered, maximum size 1.4 × 1.2 cm. In the upper pole of the right kidney, there are two hypodense round homogeneous lesions, maximum size up to 1.0 cm in diameter, density 8 HU. In the liver, there are two hypodense round homogeneous lesions, maximum size up to 1.1 cm in diameter, density -8 HU (segment 2).
val_impression:
1. Micronodule in the left upper lobe, stable compared with CT of the chest from July 26, 2019. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended. Calcified nodule in the left lower lobe, not requiring follow-up.

2. Compressive total atelectasis of the right middle lobe and subtotal atelectasis of the right lower lobe noted due to right hemidiaphragmatic elevation. Negative dynamics, atelectasis of the right middle lobe was not previously seen. Traction bronchiectasis in the right lower lobe. Pleural calcifications.

3. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic calcification. 

4. Cholelithiasis. Hypodense lesions in the right kidney (likely benign cysts, not requiring follow-up). Hypodense lesions in the liver (likely benign cysts, not requiring follow-up).

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Hypoventilatory changes in the lower basal portions of the lungs.

In the subpleural portions of the lungs, areas of paraseptal emphysema persist with areas of fibrosis (more pronounced in the upper lobes of the lungs, honeycombing pattern), visually without interval change, with single air bullae (largest along the right interlobar fissure up to 1.5 × 1.4 cm, without interval change).

In the lung parenchyma, single solid nodules are present, in series 5 as follows:
In the right upper lobe:
* Nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 93), without interval change.
In the right lower lobe:
* Nodule measuring up to 7 mm (image 121), without interval change.
In the left lower lobe:
* Nodule measuring up to 1.1 × 0.9 cm with lobulated margins (image 74), adjacent to the interlobar fissure, not previously identified.

No pleural effusion. In the posterior basal portions, an area of costal pleural thickening persists with numerous large calcifications within it; an area of calcification of the right interlobar fissure, visually without interval change.

The trachea and main bronchi are patent, with focal wall calcifications. The transverse tracheal lumen is mildly narrowed and the anteroposterior dimension is widened (saber-sheath trachea), visually without interval change.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Moderate coronary artery calcification. Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Multiple healing rib fractures (more on the left). Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with small osteophytes. Decreased height and wedge deformity of the T7 vertebral body up to 33% without spinal canal narrowing at this level. Decreased height of the C5-C7 vertebral bodies up to 45%. Mild decreased density (+145 HU) of the T12 vertebral bone tissue, findings of osteopenia.

Included upper abdomen: Small calcifications partially included within the scan field at the level of the upper and middle calyx groups of the right kidney up to 3 mm.
doc_impression:
1. Solid nodule measuring 1.1 × 0.9 cm in the left lower lobe, not previously identified. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, short-interval CT follow-up (in 3 months) is recommended, and consider positron emission tomography CT and/or tissue sampling. Other single solid lung nodules show no interval change.

2. CT findings of pulmonary emphysema with severe fibrotic changes, and saber-sheath trachea, findings of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. Right pleural thickening with areas of calcification, which may correspond to postinflammatory changes, visually without interval change.

3. Moderate coronary artery calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Aortic valve calcification.

5. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

6. Wedge deformity of the T7 and C5-C7 vertebral bodies (without findings of significant spinal canal narrowing at this level) which may be a sign of osteoporosis, and CT findings of osteopenia at T12. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with small osteophytes. Multiple healing rib fractures bilaterally.

7. Calcifications partially included in the region of the right renal calyces (calculi versus vascular calcifications or calcifications of sinus elements). Given partial visualization, reliable assessment is limited.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Coarse peripheral reticular interstitial opacities with some honeycombing and traction bronchiectasis, most pronounced in the upper lobes, with mild interval worsening compared to March 24, 2023. Areas of paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema. 
In the lung parenchyma, a few solid nodules are present, in series 5 as follows:
In the right upper lobe:
* Nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 93), without interval change.
In the right lower lobe:
* Nodule measuring up to 7 mm (image 121), without interval change.
In the left lower lobe:
* Nodule measuring up to 1.1 × 0.9 cm with lobulated margins (image 74), adjacent to the interlobar fissure, not previously identified.
No pleural effusion. In the posterior basal portions, an area of costal pleural thickening persists with numerous large calcifications; an area of calcification of the right interlobar fissure, without interval change.
The trachea and main bronchi are patent, with focal wall calcifications. The transverse tracheal lumen is mildly narrowed and the anteroposterior dimension is widened (saber-sheath trachea), visually without interval change. The walls of large and segmental bronchi are thickened.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy. Dilated, thin-walled esophagus.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Moderate coronary artery calcification. Mitral valve calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Multiple healing rib fractures. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine and shoulder joints, thoracic scoliosis. Severe degenerative changes of the cervical spine. Decreased height and wedge deformity of the T7 vertebral body up to 33% without spinal canal narrowing at this level. Mild decreased density (+145 HU) of the T12 vertebral bone tissue, findings of osteopenia.

Included upper abdomen: Small calculi of the right kidney up to 3 mm, no hydronephrosis.
val_impression:
1. Solid nodule measuring 1.1 × 0.9 cm in the left lower lobe, not previously identified. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, short-interval CT follow-up (in 3 months) is recommended, and consider positron emission tomography CT and/or tissue sampling. Other solid nodules show no interval change.

2. Pulmonary emphysema and saber-sheath trachea (findings of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease), interstitial fibrotic changes without  basal predominance. Right pleural thickening with calcification, which may correspond to postinflammatory changes, without interval change.

3. Moderate coronary artery calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Mitral valve calcification. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Wedge deformity of the T7 vertebral body, which may be a sign of osteoporosis. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine and shoulder joints, thoracic scoliosis. Severe degenerative changes of the cervical spine. Multiple healing rib fractures bilaterally.

5. Small calculi of the right kidney up to 3 mm, no hydronephrosis.
